contest.title: European Championship 2025 - Online Mirror (Unrated, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: construct a common supersequence of all required words while avoiding t as a subsequence.

Contest editorial page exists, but the expanded tutorial text for F was not available in the text snapshot; the recorded solution is based on statement-derived reasoning and brute-force sanity checks on small cases.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu candidate source; not used in this review because no solution text was available through the accessible page snapshot.

annotation.summary: Construct a string that contains every required word as a subsequence but does not contain the forbidden word t as a subsequence.
annotation.constraints: The total length of required words and |t| are up to 2e5; the constructed answer may have length up to 1e6, so a linear greedy construction is suitable.
annotation.core_idea: Track the current unmatched character of every required word and the greedy matched prefix length p of t in the output. If some current character is not t[p], output such a character and advance every word currently waiting for it; this does not advance the forbidden automaton. Only when all unfinished words currently require t[p] is any common supersequence forced to output t[p], so advance p and remove that character from all waiting words. If this would finish t, no valid name exists; otherwise the produced string is valid.
annotation.complexity: O(total input length + alphabet * output length) with simple buckets, O(total input length) memory.
tricks: Maintain the frontier characters of all required subsequences.
Avoid advancing the forbidden subsequence automaton unless every unfinished word forces the next forbidden character.
Batch-advance all words whose current required character equals the emitted character.

solution_variants.name: frontier-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Keep queues of words by their next required character. Repeatedly emit a currently needed character different from the next forbidden character if possible; otherwise emit the forced forbidden character and move the forbidden pointer. Failure occurs exactly when the forbidden pointer reaches |t|.
solution_variants.complexity: O(total required length + 26 * answer length) time, O(total required length) memory.

tags.evidence: The whole problem is about satisfying required subsequences while avoiding a forbidden subsequence.
tags.description: Problems where subsequence containment, common supersequences, forbidden subsequences, or greedy subsequence matching are central to the solution.
tags.created_reason: Existing string tags cover automata, borders, palindromes, and segmentation, but not subsequence/supersequence constraints as a reusable retrieval concept.

tags.evidence: The construction greedily emits any safe frontier character and only emits the next forbidden character when it is forced by all unfinished words.
Avoiding t is tracked by the matched-prefix state of the subsequence automaton for t.
